titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 590

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=932 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.713299+00 | translation moa=933 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.721817+00

p.cl: . W . dei gratia rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis Hominibus Totius terre sue Clericis et Laicis Salutem .

Sciant pre
sentes et futuri me concessisse et Hac Carta mea confirmasse renuntiationem et abiurationem quam . Willelmus . de golin
cum Nicholao . et Iohanne fratribus suis et Emme matertera sua pro se et tota cognatione sua fecit de uilla ma
ioris lumesdene cum omnibus ad eam spectantibus . et de iure quod in eadem uilla habuit . pro una Carucata ter
re ei et Heredibus suis in territorio de Swintun a Priore et Conuentui Dunelmensi . concessa . Quare
uolo et firmiter precipio . ut renuntiatio et abiuratio illa facta firmiter in posterum obseruetur . sicut Car
ta ipsius Willelmi . testatur . saluo seruitio meo de predictis terris .

Testibus . Willelmo . Episcopo Sancti . Andree . Philippo
de Valoniis . Camerario . meo . Roberto . de Londoniis . filio . meo . Willelmo . Cumin . Alexandro vicecomite de striuelin .
William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have allowed, and by this my charter to have made firm, the renuntiation and abjuration which William of Gullane with Nicholas and John his brothers and Emma his maternal aunt made for himself and his entire kindred of the toun of greater Lumsdaine with everything belonging to it, and that which he lawfully held in the same toun, in return for one ploughgate of land granted to him and his heirs in the territory of Swinton by the prior and convent of Durham. Wherefore I will and resolutely instruct that the renuntiation and abjuration made by him be strictly obeserved in future, just as William's own charter bears witness, saving my service from the aforesaid lands.
As witnesses: William bishop of St Andrews, Philip de Valognes my chamberlain, Robert of London my son, William Comyn, Alexander the sheriff of Stirling.
At Wedale on the 16th day of May.
